cl:composting-rate-describable.statement: For all 59 NYC community districts, CY2021–present residential-organics composting rates (diversion and capture) are computable from DSNY Monthly Tonnage (ebb7-mvp5) without structural data gaps.

am:compostable-fraction.statement: The citywide residential compostable fraction is 35.8% of total collected tonnage, per the 2023 DSNY Waste Characterization Study. Applied uniformly across all 59 districts because no per-district breakdown is published. This is the denominator for the capture rate; its uniformity is a named caveat, not a silent default.

am:yard-waste-included.statement: The organics numerator sums resorganicstons + leavesorganictons + xmastreetons + otherorganicstons. Yard waste is included because the claim concerns total diversion, not curbside food only. Seasonal patterns are treated as honest variation, not noise to strip.

jd:data-sufficient.decision: Is ebb7-mvp5 sufficient to describe per-district composting rates?
jd:data-sufficient.chosen: yes — diversion rate is a direct quotient; capture rate requires the compostable-fraction assumption
jd:data-sufficient.grounding: 708 district-months (59 districts × 12 months CY2025) with zero structural nulls in tonnage columns. Month field parsed from 'YYYY / MM' text via regex; all 59 communitydistrict values stable across dataset. Numeric coercion handles Socrata text formatting.
jd:data-sufficient.rationale: 708 district-months (59 districts × 12 months CY2025) with zero structural nulls in tonnage columns. Month field parsed from 'YYYY / MM' text via regex; all 59 communitydistrict values stable across dataset. Numeric coercion handles Socrata text formatting.

jd:fraction-appropriate.decision: Is the 2023 DSNY Waste Characterization Study 35.8% figure appropriate as the capture-rate denominator?
jd:fraction-appropriate.chosen: yes, as an explicitly named assumption with stated caveat
jd:fraction-appropriate.grounding: Only published citywide figure for NYC residential compostable fraction. Source: DSNY 2023 Waste Characterization Study. Caveat documented as assumption node: per-district breakdown unavailable; uniformity assumption is explicit, not silent.
jd:fraction-appropriate.rationale: Only published citywide figure for NYC residential compostable fraction. Source: DSNY 2023 Waste Characterization Study. Caveat documented as assumption node: per-district breakdown unavailable; uniformity assumption is explicit, not silent.

jd:mandate-effect-describable.decision: Is a post-mandate composting rate change visible and describable from ebb7-mvp5?
jd:mandate-effect-describable.chosen: yes, as a describing claim; not an attribution claim
jd:mandate-effect-describable.grounding: Diversion rate rises after Oct 6 2024 across most districts. CY2025 is a rollout transient — districts are still climbing ~4× over the year — so the data describes a system mid-rollout, not equilibrium. The describing claim passes; the attribution claim (jd:attribution-cantTell) does not.
jd:mandate-effect-describable.rationale: Diversion rate rises after Oct 6 2024 across most districts. CY2025 is a rollout transient — districts are still climbing ~4× over the year — so the data describes a system mid-rollout, not equilibrium. The describing claim passes; the attribution claim (jd:attribution-cantTell) does not.

jd:attribution-cantTell.decision: Can the observed post-Oct-2024 composting rate increase be causally attributed to the residential mandate?
jd:attribution-cantTell.grounding: Rollout transient (CY2025 mid-climb); seasonal confounders uncontrolled; no counterfactual district available; cannot distinguish mandate effect from organic growth in the universal-availability rollout. The increase is real and describable; its cause is not identifiable from this data alone.
jd:attribution-cantTell.rationale: Rollout transient (CY2025 mid-climb); seasonal confounders uncontrolled; no counterfactual district available; cannot distinguish mandate effect from organic growth in the universal-availability rollout. The increase is real and describable; its cause is not identifiable from this data alone.

ev:dsny-tonnage.finding: 708 district-months of residential-organics tonnage (59 districts × CY2021-present); zero structural nulls in resorganicstons, leavesorganictons, xmastreetons, otherorganicstons; month parsed from 'YYYY / MM' text; 25,060 rows total; diversion rate computable as direct quotient.

ev:waste-char-2023.finding: Citywide residential compostable fraction = 35.8% of total collected tonnage. Basis for capture-rate denominator. No per-district breakdown published in this study.
